stance: Risk-on, momentum-led but quality-tilted: lean into rising analyst-revision names, prune the dead post-demerger stubs and falling-revision laggards.
experience_note: No last_round_review exists, so I have pre-registered theses, not post-hoc rationalisations. What I am watching: (1) whether the ARM tilt actually pays — if the rising-revision basket (ONGC, NMDC, LICI, Muthoot, Coforge, Asian Paints, Grasim, Solar, Deepak Nitrite) does not beat the falling-revision names I cut (Tata Motors, RBL, Adani Energy Sol, Onesource) over the next 1-3 months, the ARM edge was noise this round and I will de-size it next time; (2) whether dropping the Vedanta stubs costs me — if those spin entities re-rate sharply post-demerger I was wrong to treat 'no signal' as 'no value'; (3) since z_mom is flat market-wide, I am NOT relying on price momentum this round and would change my mind if a clear momentum dispersion re-emerges. I would also revisit if net-active flow data (currently null) turns on and contradicts the ARM picture.
